cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • bare `apk add` redirects to sandbox_install
notes: note: A model ran `apk add curl` directly in sandbox_exec. apk needs root, so the unprivileged agent shell fails to lock the package database (exit 1). The self-heal hint must fire and point at sandbox_install (manager:"apk") so the refresh + retry + cross-agent serialization harness applies instead of a bare bash install.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • correctly-escaped one-liner stays silent
notes: note: A correctly-escaped `python3 -c 'print(1)'` exits 0 with no parse signature. The escape hint must stay silent so working commands are never nagged.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • multi-line python3 -c parse error surfaces escape hint
notes: note: Reproduces the canonical local-model failure: a multi-line script embedded in `python3 -c "…"` whose JSON→shell→code escaping broke, so bash mis-parsed the body and exited with `syntax error near unexpected token '('`. The self-heal hint must fire and point at sandbox_write_file (write the script to a file, then run it with sandbox_exec).

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • bare `npm install` redirects to sandbox_install
notes: note: A model ran `npm install express` directly in the agent home instead of sandbox_install (manager:"npm"). Bare npm in the home is exactly what produced the `Tracker idealTree already exists` failures (here exit 1). The self-heal hint must fire and point at sandbox_install so the isolated node workspace + recovery harness applies.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • bare `pip install` redirects to sandbox_install
notes: note: A model ran `pip install numpy` directly in sandbox_exec instead of sandbox_install (manager:"pip"). Bare pip skips the per-agent venv bootstrap + retry harness and routinely fails (here exit 1). The self-heal hint must fire and point at sandbox_install so the venv-scoped install path applies.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • shell syntax error without an interpreter -c/-e stays silent
notes: note: A shell-parse error in an ordinary command (no `python -c` / `node -e`) is unrelated to the inline-code trap. The targeted hint would be misleading, so it must not fire.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • stray leading quote surfaces unbalanced-quote hint
notes: note: Reproduces the observed failure where the model wrapped the whole command in a stray leading single quote (`'curl -s "…"`), so bash read to end-of-input looking for the closing `'` and reported `unexpected EOF while looking for matching '`. The self-heal hint must flag the unbalanced quote.

cases.label: sandbox_diagnostics • unterminated heredoc surfaces write-file hint
notes: note: A `cat <<EOF` whose delimiter is never closed makes bash read to end-of-input and warn `here-document … delimited by end-of-file`. The self-heal hint must point at sandbox_write_file for multi-line content instead of a shell heredoc.
